metadata.name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5
metadata.description: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations. NIST SP 800-53 provides a catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems and organizations to protect organizational operations, assets, individuals, and the Nation.
metadata.source_organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
metadata.classification_reasoning: NIST 800-53 is a compliance framework providing a catalog of security controls. While not requiring formal certification, it is mandated for federal systems under FISMA and serves as the basis for FedRAMP authorization.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide a comprehensive catalog of security and privacy controls for information systems and organizations, supporting risk management processes across the federal government and private sector.
metadata.framework_scope: All federal information systems and organizations, as well as private sector organizations seeking robust security and privacy controls.
framework_objectives: Protect individuals and the Nation from cyber threats
Provide a structured approach to selecting security controls
Support compliance with FISMA, FedRAMP, and other regulations
metadata.target_audience: Federal agencies, government contractors, cloud service providers, security architects, CISOs, and compliance officers
metadata.regulatory_authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), U.S. Department of Commerce
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Federal agencies face audit findings and loss of authority to operate (ATO). Contractors may lose government contracts.
adoption_approach: Select baseline controls from Low/Moderate/High baselines
Tailor controls based on organizational risk assessment
Monitor controls on an ongoing basis

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an access control policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance. The organization also develops procedures to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and associated controls. The policy and procedures are reviewed and updated at organization-defined frequencies.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.description: Define and manage information system accounts including identifying account types, establishing conditions for group membership, assigning account managers, and managing the account lifecycle.
controls.full_text: The organization manages information system accounts by identifying and selecting account types, assigning account managers, establishing conditions for group and role membership, specifying authorized users and access privileges, requiring approvals for account creation, creating/enabling/modifying/disabling/removing accounts, monitoring account usage, and reviewing accounts for compliance.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources.
controls.full_text: The information system enforces approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies. Access enforcement mechanisms include access control lists, access control matrices, and cryptographic mechanisms.

controls.title: Information Flow Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between connected systems.
controls.full_text: The information system enforces approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between interconnected systems based on information flow control policies. Information flow control regulates where information can travel versus who can access the information.

controls.title: Separation of Duties
controls.description: Identify and document duties of individuals requiring separation and define system access authorizations to support separation of duties.
controls.full_text: The organization separates duties of individuals as necessary to prevent malicious activity without collusion, identifies duties requiring separation, defines information system access authorizations to support separation of duties, and ensures that no single individual has the ability to perform all critical functions.
evidence_requirements: Role definitions with incompatible duties identified

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.
controls.full_text: The organization employs the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) which are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks. Organizations employ least privilege for specific duties and information systems, including limiting administrative access to privileged functions.

controls.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
controls.description: Enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user and take defined actions when the maximum is exceeded.
controls.full_text: The information system enforces a limit of organization-defined consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during an organization-defined time period, and automatically locks the account or delays next logon prompt when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.

controls.title: System Use Notification
controls.description: Display an approved system use notification message before granting access.
controls.full_text: The information system displays an approved system use notification message or banner before granting access, providing privacy and security notices consistent with applicable policies, standards, and guidelines. The notification includes conditions of use, monitoring statements, and authorized access descriptions.

controls.title: Device Lock
controls.description: Prevent further access to the system by initiating a device lock after a defined period of inactivity.
controls.full_text: The information system prevents further access to the system by initiating a session lock after an organization-defined time period of inactivity or upon receiving a request from a user. The session lock is retained until the user re-establishes access using established identification and authentication procedures.

controls.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
controls.description: Identify user actions that can be performed without identification and authentication.
controls.full_text: The organization identifies and documents specific user actions that can be performed on the information system without identification or authentication consistent with organizational missions and business functions.

controls.title: Remote Access
controls.description: Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration requirements, and connection requirements for each type of remote access allowed.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes and documents usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed. The organization authorizes remote access to the information system prior to allowing such connections, enforces requirements, and monitors for unauthorized remote connections.

controls.title: Wireless Access
controls.description: Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for wireless access.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for wireless access. Authorizes wireless access prior to allowing such connections and monitors for unauthorized wireless access.

controls.title: Access Control for Mobile Devices
controls.description: Establish usage restrictions and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes usage restrictions, configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices. Authorizes the connection of mobile devices to organizational information systems.

controls.title: Use of External Systems
controls.description: Establish terms and conditions for authorized individuals to access the system from external systems.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes terms and conditions, consistent with any trust relationships established with other organizations owning, operating, and/or maintaining external information systems, allowing authorized individuals to access the information system from external systems and process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information using external systems.
evidence_requirements: Risk assessment for external system use

controls.title: Publicly Accessible Content
controls.description: Designate individuals authorized to post information onto publicly accessible systems and review content before posting.
controls.full_text: The organization designates individuals authorized to post information onto publicly accessible information systems, trains authorized individuals, reviews proposed content before posting, reviews posted content for non-public information at organization-defined frequency, and removes non-public information if discovered.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate security awareness and training policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a security awareness and training policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination, and compliance. Procedures facilitate implementation of the policy and associated controls.

controls.title: Literacy Training and Awareness
controls.description: Provide security and privacy literacy training to system users as part of initial training and at defined frequency.
controls.full_text: The organization provides basic security and privacy literacy training to information system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors) as part of initial training for new users, when required by information system changes, and at an organization-defined frequency thereafter. Training includes recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat, social engineering, and phishing.

controls.title: Role-Based Training
controls.description: Provide role-based security and privacy training to personnel with assigned security roles before authorizing access.
controls.full_text: The organization provides role-based security-related training to personnel with assigned security roles and responsibilities before authorizing access to the information system or performing assigned duties, when required by information system changes, and at organization-defined frequency thereafter.

controls.title: Training Records
controls.description: Document and monitor information system security training activities.
controls.full_text: The organization documents and monitors individual information system security training activities including basic security awareness training and specific information system security training. Training records are retained for an organization-defined time period.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate audit and accountability policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an audit and accountability policy and associated procedures.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.description: Identify events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function.
controls.full_text: The organization determines that the information system is capable of auditing organization-defined events, coordinates the audit function with other entities, provides a rationale for why the auditable events are deemed adequate, and reviews and updates the auditable events on an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.description: Ensure audit records contain sufficient information to establish what occurred, when, where, the source, and outcome.
controls.full_text: The information system generates audit records containing information that establishes what type of event occurred, when the event occurred, where the event occurred, the source of the event, the outcome of the event, and the identity of any individuals, subjects, or objects/entities associated with the event.

controls.title: Audit Log Storage Capacity
controls.description: Allocate audit log storage capacity and configure auditing to reduce the likelihood of storage exhaustion.
controls.full_text: The organization allocates audit record storage capacity in accordance with organization-defined audit record storage requirements and configures auditing to reduce the likelihood of such capacity being exceeded.

controls.title: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
controls.description: Alert personnel in the event of an audit logging process failure and take defined additional actions.
controls.full_text: The information system alerts designated organizational officials in the event of an audit processing failure and takes organization-defined additional actions (e.g., shutdown, overwrite oldest records, stop generating records).

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.description: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.
controls.full_text: The organization reviews and analyzes information system audit records at an organization-defined frequency for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity, reports findings to designated organizational officials, and adjusts the level of audit review, analysis, and reporting when there is a change in risk.

controls.title: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
controls.description: Provide an audit record reduction and report generation capability.
controls.full_text: The information system provides an audit record reduction and report generation capability that supports on-demand audit review, analysis, and reporting requirements and after-the-fact investigations of security incidents.
evidence_requirements: SIEM or log analysis tool documentation

controls.title: Time Stamps
controls.description: Use internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records.
controls.full_text: The information system uses internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records and records time stamps that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) or Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.description: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.
controls.full_text: The information system protects audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion. The organization authorizes access to management of audit logging functionality to only a subset of privileged users.
evidence_requirements: Privileged access to audit tools documentation

controls.title: Audit Record Retention
controls.description: Retain audit records for a defined period to provide support for after-the-fact investigations.
controls.full_text: The organization retains audit records for an organization-defined time period to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of security incidents and to meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.description: Provide audit record generation capability for auditable events at all system components.
controls.full_text: The information system provides audit record generation capability for the auditable events defined in AU-2 at all information system and network components where audit capability is deployed, allows designated organizational personnel to select which auditable events are to be audited by specific components, and generates audit records.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates security assessment and authorization policy and procedures.

controls.title: Control Assessments
controls.description: Develop a security and privacy assessment plan, assess controls, produce assessment report, and provide results.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a security assessment plan, assesses security controls in the information system and its environment of operation at an organization-defined frequency to determine the extent to which the controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome. Assessment results are documented in a security assessment report.

controls.title: Information Exchange
controls.description: Approve and manage information exchange between the system and other systems using interconnection security agreements.
controls.full_text: The organization approves and manages the exchange of information between the information system and other information systems using interconnection security agreements, documents interface characteristics, security requirements, and the nature of information communicated.

controls.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
controls.description: Develop and update a plan of action and milestones for the system to document planned remedial actions.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a plan of action and milestones for the information system to document the organization's planned remedial actions to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during security control assessments and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Authorization
controls.description: Assign a senior official as the authorizing official for the system and ensure authorization before operation.
controls.full_text: The organization assigns a senior-level executive or manager as the authorizing official for the information system, ensures the authorizing official authorizes the information system for processing before commencing operations, and updates the authorization on an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.description: Develop a continuous monitoring strategy and program including metrics, monitoring frequency, and ongoing assessments.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a continuous monitoring strategy and implements a continuous monitoring program that includes establishing organization-defined metrics, monitoring security control effectiveness, ongoing assessments, and reporting the security state of the system.

controls.title: Internal System Connections
controls.description: Authorize internal connections of system components and document connection characteristics.
controls.full_text: The organization authorizes internal connections of information system components or classes of components to the information system and documents, for each internal connection, the interface characteristics, security requirements, and the nature of the information communicated.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate configuration management policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a configuration management policy and procedures.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.description: Develop, document, and maintain a current baseline configuration of the information system.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and maintains under configuration control a current baseline configuration of the information system, reviews and updates the baseline at an organization-defined frequency and as an integral part of system component installations and upgrades.

controls.title: Configuration Change Control
controls.description: Determine and document types of changes under configuration control, review and approve changes, and track implementation.
controls.full_text: The organization determines the types of changes to the information system that are configuration-controlled, reviews proposed configuration-controlled changes to the information system, approves or disapproves changes with explicit consideration for security impact analyses, documents configuration change decisions, implements approved changes, and retains records.

controls.title: Impact Analyses
controls.description: Analyze changes to the information system to determine potential security and privacy impacts prior to change implementation.
controls.full_text: The organization analyzes changes to the information system to determine potential security impacts prior to change implementation. Security impact analyses are conducted by individuals with the required skills and technical expertise.

controls.title: Access Restrictions for Change
controls.description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes.
controls.full_text: The organization defines, documents, approves, and enforces physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to the information system. Only qualified and authorized individuals obtain access to system components for purposes of initiating changes.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.description: Establish and document mandatory configuration settings for IT products using security configuration checklists.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes and documents configuration settings for information technology products employed within the information system using organization-defined security configuration checklists, implements the configuration settings, and identifies, documents, and approves any deviations from established configuration settings.

controls.title: Least Functionality
controls.description: Configure the system to provide only mission-essential capabilities and restrict use of prohibited functions, ports, and services.
controls.full_text: The organization configures the information system to provide only essential capabilities and prohibits or restricts the use of functions, ports, protocols, and/or services as defined by the organization.

controls.title: System Component Inventory
controls.description: Develop and document an inventory of system components that accurately reflects the system and is at the required granularity.
controls.full_text: The organization develops and documents an inventory of information system components that accurately reflects the current information system, includes all components within the authorization boundary, is at the level of granularity deemed necessary for tracking and reporting, and reviews/updates the inventory at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Software Usage Restrictions
controls.description: Use software and associated documentation in accordance with contract agreements and copyright laws.
controls.full_text: The organization uses software and associated documentation in accordance with contract agreements and copyright laws, tracks the use of software and associated documentation, and controls and documents the use of peer-to-peer file sharing technology.

controls.title: User-Installed Software
controls.description: Establish and enforce policies governing the installation of software by users.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes and enforces policies governing the installation of software by users, and monitors policy compliance at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate contingency planning policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a contingency planning policy and procedures.

controls.title: Contingency Plan
controls.description: Develop, distribute, and maintain a contingency plan for the information system.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a contingency plan for the information system that identifies essential missions and business functions, provides recovery objectives, restoration priorities, and metrics, addresses contingency roles and responsibilities, is reviewed and approved, and distributed to key personnel.

controls.title: Contingency Training
controls.description: Provide contingency training to system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: The organization provides contingency training to information system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities within a defined time period of assuming a contingency role, when required by system changes, and at an organization-defined frequency thereafter.

controls.title: Contingency Plan Testing
controls.description: Test the contingency plan at a defined frequency using defined tests to determine effectiveness.
controls.full_text: The organization tests the contingency plan for the information system at an organization-defined frequency using organization-defined tests to determine the effectiveness of the plan and the organizational readiness to execute the plan. Test results are reviewed and the plan is updated based on findings.
evidence_requirements: Plan updates based on test findings

controls.title: Alternate Storage Site
controls.description: Establish an alternate storage site and initiate agreements to permit storage and retrieval of backup information.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes an alternate storage site, including necessary agreements, to permit the storage and retrieval of information system backup information. The alternate storage site is separated from the primary storage site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

controls.title: Alternate Processing Site
controls.description: Establish an alternate processing site and initiate agreements for essential mission operations.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes an alternate processing site, including necessary agreements, to permit the transfer and resumption of information system operations for essential missions/business functions within an organization-defined time period.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.description: Conduct backups of user-level, system-level, and system documentation at defined frequency.
controls.full_text: The organization conducts backups of user-level information, system-level information, and information system documentation (including security-related documentation) at an organization-defined frequency consistent with recovery time and recovery point objectives.

controls.title: System Recovery and Reconstitution
controls.description: Provide for the recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state after a disruption or failure.
controls.full_text: The organization provides for the recovery and reconstitution of the information system to a known state after a disruption, compromise, or failure. Recovery procedures include restoring systems to operational status and reconstituting system components.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate identification and authentication policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an identification and authentication policy and procedures.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users, or processes acting on behalf of organizational users.
controls.full_text: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of organizational users). Implementation includes multi-factor authentication for privileged and non-privileged accounts accessing the system.

controls.title: Device Identification and Authentication
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate devices before establishing a connection.
controls.full_text: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates specific and/or types of devices before establishing a local, remote, or network connection using bidirectional authentication between devices.

controls.title: Identifier Management
controls.description: Manage information system identifiers by receiving authorization, selecting and assigning identifiers, and preventing reuse.
controls.full_text: The organization manages information system identifiers by receiving authorization from designated organizational officials to assign identifiers, selecting identifiers that identify individuals/groups/roles/devices, assigning identifiers to intended parties, preventing reuse for a defined period, and disabling identifiers after a defined period of inactivity.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.description: Manage information system authenticators by verifying user identity, establishing initial content, and ensuring sufficient strength.
controls.full_text: The organization manages information system authenticators by verifying the identity of the individual/group/role/device as part of the initial authenticator distribution, establishing initial authenticator content, ensuring authenticators have sufficient strength, establishing and implementing administrative procedures for initial distribution/lost/compromised/damaged authenticators, changing default content, and protecting authenticator content from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

controls.title: Authentication Feedback
controls.description: Obscure feedback of authentication information during the authentication process.
controls.full_text: The information system obscures feedback of authentication information during the authentication process to protect the information from possible exploitation/use by unauthorized individuals.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users or processes acting on their behalf.
controls.full_text: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates non-organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users). This includes contractors, guests, and external system users.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate incident response policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an incident response policy and procedures.

controls.title: Incident Response Training
controls.description: Provide incident response training to system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: The organization provides incident response training to information system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities within a defined time period and at an organization-defined frequency thereafter.

controls.title: Incident Response Testing
controls.description: Test the incident response capability using defined tests at defined frequency.
controls.full_text: The organization tests the incident response capability for the information system at an organization-defined frequency using organization-defined tests to determine incident response effectiveness and documents results.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.description: Implement an incident handling capability including preparation, detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
controls.full_text: The organization implements an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery. The organization coordinates incident handling activities with contingency planning activities and incorporates lessons learned.

controls.title: Incident Monitoring
controls.description: Track and document information system security incidents.
controls.full_text: The organization tracks and documents information system security incidents on an ongoing basis.

controls.title: Incident Reporting
controls.description: Require personnel to report suspected security incidents and report incidents to defined authorities.
controls.full_text: The organization requires personnel to report suspected security and privacy incidents to the organizational incident response capability within an organization-defined time period and reports security incident information to designated authorities.

controls.title: Incident Response Assistance
controls.description: Provide an incident response support resource that offers advice and assistance to users.
controls.full_text: The organization provides an incident response support resource, integral to the organizational incident response capability, that offers advice and assistance to users for the handling and reporting of security incidents.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Develop an incident response plan that provides a roadmap for implementing the incident response capability.
controls.full_text: The organization develops an incident response plan that provides the organization with a roadmap for implementing its incident response capability, describes the structure and organization of the incident response capability, provides a high-level approach for how the incident response capability fits into the overall organization, and is reviewed and approved.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system maintenance policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an information system maintenance policy and procedures.

controls.title: Controlled Maintenance
controls.description: Schedule, perform, document, and review records of maintenance and repairs on system components.
controls.full_text: The organization schedules, performs, documents, and reviews records of maintenance and repairs on information system components in accordance with manufacturer or vendor specifications and/or organizational requirements.

controls.title: Maintenance Tools
controls.description: Approve, control, and monitor information system maintenance tools.
controls.full_text: The organization approves, controls, and monitors the use of information system maintenance tools and maintains maintenance tools on an ongoing basis.

controls.title: Nonlocal Maintenance
controls.description: Approve and monitor nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic activities.
controls.full_text: The organization authorizes, monitors, and controls nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic activities, and ensures equipment used for remote maintenance is sanitized or inspected before and after use.

controls.title: Maintenance Personnel
controls.description: Establish a process for maintenance personnel authorization and maintain a list of authorized maintenance organizations.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes a process for maintenance personnel authorization and maintains a list of authorized maintenance organizations or personnel. Personnel without required access authorizations, clearances, or formal access approvals are supervised during maintenance activities.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate media protection policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a media protection policy and procedures.

controls.title: Media Access
controls.description: Restrict access to digital and non-digital media to defined personnel or roles.
controls.full_text: The organization restricts access to organization-defined types of digital and/or non-digital media to organization-defined personnel or roles.

controls.title: Media Marking
controls.description: Mark information system media indicating the distribution limitations and handling caveats.
controls.full_text: The organization marks information system media indicating the distribution limitations, handling caveats, and applicable security markings of the information.

controls.title: Media Storage
controls.description: Physically control and securely store digital and non-digital media within controlled areas.
controls.full_text: The organization physically controls and securely stores organization-defined types of digital and/or non-digital media within organization-defined controlled areas using organization-defined security safeguards.

controls.title: Media Transport
controls.description: Protect and control digital and non-digital media during transport outside of controlled areas.
controls.full_text: The organization protects and controls organization-defined types of media during transport outside of controlled areas using organization-defined security safeguards. Activities associated with transport include the physical movement of media.

controls.title: Media Sanitization
controls.description: Sanitize information system media prior to disposal, release out of organizational control, or release for reuse.
controls.full_text: The organization sanitizes information system media, both digital and non-digital, prior to disposal, release out of organizational control, or release for reuse using organization-defined sanitization techniques and procedures in accordance with applicable standards.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate physical and environmental protection policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a physical and environmental protection policy and procedures.

controls.title: Physical Access Authorizations
controls.description: Develop, approve, and maintain a list of individuals with authorized access to facilities.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, approves, and maintains a list of individuals with authorized access to the facility where the information system resides, issues authorization credentials, reviews the access list and authorization credentials at an organization-defined frequency, and removes individuals from the access list when access is no longer required.

controls.title: Physical Access Control
controls.description: Enforce physical access authorizations at entry/exit points by verifying individual access and controlling ingress/egress.
controls.full_text: The organization enforces physical access authorizations at defined entry/exit points to the facility by verifying individual access authorizations before granting access and controlling ingress/egress using physical access control systems, guards, or both.

controls.title: Monitoring Physical Access
controls.description: Monitor physical access to the facility where the information system resides to detect and respond to incidents.
controls.full_text: The organization monitors physical access to the facility where the information system resides to detect and respond to physical security incidents. Monitoring includes intrusion alarms, surveillance equipment, and guards.

controls.title: Visitor Access Records
controls.description: Maintain visitor access records to the facility and review visitor access records at defined frequency.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains visitor access records to the facility where the information system resides that include name, organization, signature, form of identification, date/time of access, purpose of visit, and name/organization of person visited. Reviews visitor access records at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Emergency Shutoff
controls.description: Provide the capability of shutting off power to the system in emergency situations.
controls.full_text: The organization provides the capability of shutting off power to the information system or individual system components in emergency situations, places emergency shutoff switches or devices in a defined location, and protects the capability from unauthorized activation.

controls.title: Fire Protection
controls.description: Employ and maintain fire suppression and detection devices/systems.
controls.full_text: The organization employs and maintains fire suppression and detection devices/systems for the information system that are supported by an independent energy source. The organization ensures that the facility undergoes fire marshal inspections at required intervals.

controls.title: Environmental Controls
controls.description: Maintain temperature and humidity levels within the facility and monitor environmental conditions.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains temperature and humidity levels within the facility where the information system resides at organization-defined acceptable levels and monitors environmental conditions at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate security planning policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a security planning policy and procedures.

controls.title: System Security and Privacy Plans
controls.description: Develop, review, update, and protect security plans that describe the system and operational environment.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, reviews, and updates security plans for the information system that describe the security categorization, the operational environment, relationships with other systems, the security requirements, and relevant overlays.

controls.title: Rules of Behavior
controls.description: Establish and make available rules describing responsibilities and expected behavior for system use.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes and makes readily available to individuals requiring access to the information system, the rules that describe their responsibilities and expected behavior with regard to information and information system usage.

controls.title: Information Security Program Plan
controls.description: Develop and disseminate an organization-wide information security program plan.
controls.full_text: The organization develops and disseminates an organization-wide information security program plan that provides an overview of the requirements for the security program and a description of the security program management controls and common controls in place or planned.

controls.title: Information Security Program Leadership Role
controls.description: Appoint a senior information security officer with the authority and responsibility for the program.
controls.full_text: The organization appoints a senior information security officer with the mission and resources to coordinate, develop, implement, and maintain an organization-wide information security program.

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy
controls.description: Develop a comprehensive strategy to manage risk to organizational operations and assets.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a comprehensive strategy to manage risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation associated with the operation and use of information systems. The risk management strategy includes risk tolerance definitions.

controls.title: Authorization Process
controls.description: Manage the security and privacy state of systems through authorization processes.
controls.full_text: The organization manages the security state of organizational information systems and the environments in which those systems operate through authorization processes. Uses a risk-based approach integrated into the SDLC.

controls.title: Mission and Business Process Definition
controls.description: Define organizational mission and business processes with consideration for information security.
controls.full_text: The organization defines mission/business processes with consideration for information security and the resulting risk to organizational operations, organizational assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate personnel security policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a personnel security policy and procedures.

controls.title: Position Risk Designation
controls.description: Assign a risk designation to all organizational positions and establish screening criteria.
controls.full_text: The organization assigns a risk designation to all organizational positions, establishes screening criteria for individuals filling those positions, and reviews and updates position risk designations at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Personnel Screening
controls.description: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to the information system.
controls.full_text: The organization screens individuals prior to authorizing access to the information system and rescreens individuals according to organization-defined conditions and at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Personnel Termination
controls.description: Upon termination, disable system access, terminate authenticators, and retrieve security-related property.
controls.full_text: The organization, upon termination of individual employment, disables information system access within an organization-defined time period, terminates/revokes any authenticators/credentials associated with the individual, conducts exit interviews, retrieves all security-related organizational property, and retains access to organizational information.

controls.title: Personnel Transfer
controls.description: Review and confirm ongoing operational need for access when personnel are reassigned or transferred.
controls.full_text: The organization reviews and confirms ongoing operational need for current logical and physical access authorizations to information systems and facilities when individuals are reassigned or transferred to other positions within the organization.

controls.title: Access Agreements
controls.description: Develop and document access agreements for organizational information systems.
controls.full_text: The organization develops and documents access agreements for organizational information systems, reviews and updates the access agreements at an organization-defined frequency, and ensures individuals sign access agreements before being granted access.

controls.title: External Personnel Security
controls.description: Establish personnel security requirements for third-party providers and monitor compliance.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes personnel security requirements including security roles and responsibilities for third-party providers, requires third-party providers to comply with personnel security policies and procedures, documents personnel security requirements, and monitors third-party provider compliance.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate PII processing and transparency policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates PII processing and transparency policy and procedures.

controls.title: Authority to Process PII
controls.description: Determine and document the legal authority that permits the processing of PII.
controls.full_text: The organization determines and documents the legal authority that permits the collection, use, maintenance, and sharing of PII, specified in the applicable privacy act, and ensures that PII processing is consistent with that authority.

controls.title: PII Processing Purposes
controls.description: Identify and document the purposes for processing PII.
controls.full_text: The organization identifies and documents the purpose(s) for which PII is collected, used, maintained, and shared. The organization describes those purposes in the relevant privacy documentation and ensures that processing is compatible with the identified purpose(s).

controls.title: Consent
controls.description: Implement mechanisms to obtain consent for PII processing.
controls.full_text: The organization implements mechanisms to obtain individual consent for the processing of their PII prior to collection or within an organization-defined time period. Provides mechanisms for individuals to revoke consent.

controls.title: Privacy Notice
controls.description: Provide effective notice to the public and individuals about PII processing activities.
controls.full_text: The organization provides effective notice to the public and to individuals regarding its PII processing activities, including information about the authority for collecting PII, the purposes for processing, and the choices and rights available to individuals.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate risk assessment policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a risk assessment policy and procedures.

controls.title: Security Categorization
controls.description: Categorize the information system and information processed based on impact analysis.
controls.full_text: The organization categorizes information and the information system in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and standards. Documents the security categorization results in the security plan.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct risk assessments to identify threats, vulnerabilities, impact, and likelihood.
controls.full_text: The organization conducts an assessment of risk, including the likelihood and magnitude of harm, from the unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of the information system and the information it processes, stores, or transmits. The organization updates risk assessments at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.description: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the information system and hosted applications.
controls.full_text: The organization scans for vulnerabilities in the information system and hosted applications at an organization-defined frequency and when new vulnerabilities are identified, employs vulnerability scanning tools and techniques that facilitate interoperability, analyzes vulnerability scan reports and results, remediates legitimate vulnerabilities, and shares information from the vulnerability scanning process.

controls.title: Risk Response
controls.description: Respond to findings from security assessments, monitoring, and audits.
controls.full_text: The organization responds to findings from security and privacy assessments, monitoring, and audits in accordance with organizational risk tolerance.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and services acquisition policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a system and services acquisition policy and procedures.

controls.title: Allocation of Resources
controls.description: Determine, document, and allocate resources required to protect the information system.
controls.full_text: The organization determines information security and privacy requirements for the information system or information system service in mission/business process planning, determines, documents, and allocates the resources required to protect the information system, and establishes a discrete line item for information security and privacy in organizational programming and budgeting.

controls.title: System Development Life Cycle
controls.description: Manage the information system using a system development life cycle that incorporates security.
controls.full_text: The organization manages the information system using an organization-defined system development life cycle that incorporates information security considerations, defines and documents information security roles and responsibilities throughout the SDLC, identifies individuals having information security roles and responsibilities, and integrates the security risk management process into SDLC activities.

controls.title: Acquisition Process
controls.description: Include security and privacy requirements in the acquisition contract for the information system.
controls.full_text: The organization includes security functional requirements, security strength requirements, security assurance requirements, security-related documentation requirements, and requirements for protecting security-related documentation in acquisition contracts.

controls.title: Security and Privacy Engineering Principles
controls.description: Apply information system security and privacy engineering principles in system specification, design, and development.
controls.full_text: The organization applies information system security engineering principles in the specification, design, development, implementation, and modification of the information system.

controls.title: External System Services
controls.description: Require external providers to comply with security requirements and employ defined oversight processes.
controls.full_text: The organization requires that providers of external information system services comply with organizational information security requirements and employ organization-defined security controls in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and agreements.
evidence_requirements: Service level agreements with security requirements

controls.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
controls.description: Require the developer to create and implement a security assessment plan.
controls.full_text: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to create and implement a security assessment plan, perform unit/integration/system/regression testing, produce evidence of execution, and correct flaws identified during testing.

controls.title: Unsupported System Components
controls.description: Replace system components when support for components is no longer available from the developer.
controls.full_text: The organization replaces information system components when support for the components is no longer available from the developer, vendor, or manufacturer, and provides alternative sources for continued support for unsupported components.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and communications protection policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a system and communications protection policy and procedures.

controls.title: Denial-of-Service Protection
controls.description: Protect against or limit the effects of denial-of-service attacks.
controls.full_text: The information system protects against or limits the effects of denial-of-service attacks by employing organization-defined security safeguards.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.description: Monitor and control communications at external managed interfaces including key internal boundaries.
controls.full_text: The information system monitors and controls communications at the external boundary of the system and at key internal boundaries within the system. The system implements subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are logically separated from internal organizational networks.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information.
controls.full_text: The information system protects the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information using cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure and detect changes to information during transmission.
evidence_requirements: Encryption configuration for data in transit

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
controls.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys using organization-defined requirements.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes and manages cryptographic keys for required cryptography employed within the information system in accordance with organization-defined requirements for key generation, distribution, storage, access, and destruction.

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection
controls.description: Determine the cryptographic uses required and implement cryptography.
controls.full_text: The information system implements organization-defined cryptographic uses and types of cryptography required for each use in accordance with applicable federal laws, directives, policies, regulations, and standards.

controls.title: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
controls.description: Issue public key certificates under an organization-defined certificate policy or obtain certificates from an approved provider.
controls.full_text: The organization issues public key certificates under an organization-defined certificate policy or obtains public key certificates from an approved service provider.

controls.title: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Authoritative Source)
controls.description: Provide additional data origin authentication and integrity verification artifacts for authoritative DNS.
controls.full_text: The information system provides additional data origin authentication and integrity verification artifacts along with the authoritative name resolution data the system returns in response to external name/address resolution queries.

controls.title: Session Authenticity
controls.description: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.
controls.full_text: The information system protects the authenticity of communications sessions, including protection against session hijacking, man-in-the-middle attacks, and session replay.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.
controls.full_text: The information system protects the confidentiality and/or integrity of organization-defined information at rest using cryptographic mechanisms.
evidence_requirements: Encryption key management for data at rest

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and information integrity policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a system and information integrity policy and procedures.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws and install security-relevant updates.
controls.full_text: The organization identifies, reports, and corrects information system flaws, tests software and firmware updates related to flaw remediation for effectiveness and potential side effects, installs security-relevant software and firmware updates within organization-defined time periods, and incorporates flaw remediation into the configuration management process.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points.
controls.full_text: The organization employs malicious code protection mechanisms at information system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code, updates malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available, and configures mechanisms to perform periodic scans and real-time scans.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor the information system to detect attacks, unauthorized connections, and indicators of compromise.
controls.full_text: The organization monitors the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks, unauthorized local/network/remote connections, and identifies unauthorized use. The organization deploys monitoring devices strategically and heightens monitoring activity when there is an indication of increased risk.

controls.title: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives
controls.description: Receive security alerts and advisories and take appropriate actions.
controls.full_text: The organization receives information system security alerts, advisories, and directives from organization-defined external organizations on an ongoing basis, generates internal security alerts, advisories, and directives, and disseminates them to appropriate personnel.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
controls.description: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information.
controls.full_text: The organization employs integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information. The organization takes organization-defined actions when unauthorized changes are detected.

controls.title: Information Input Validation
controls.description: Check the validity of information inputs to the information system.
controls.full_text: The information system checks the validity of organization-defined information inputs. Input validation checks include syntax checks, format checks, and content checks.

controls.title: Error Handling
controls.description: Generate error messages that provide information necessary for corrective actions without revealing exploitation information.
controls.full_text: The information system generates error messages that provide information necessary for corrective actions without revealing information that could be exploited by adversaries. The system reveals error messages only to authorized personnel.

controls.title: Information Management and Retention
controls.description: Manage and retain information within the information system in accordance with applicable laws.
controls.full_text: The organization manages and retains information within the information system and information output from the system in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, guidelines, and operational requirements.

controls.title: Memory Protection
controls.description: Implement organization-defined security safeguards to protect memory from unauthorized code execution.
controls.full_text: The information system implements organization-defined security safeguards to protect its memory from unauthorized code execution. Examples include data execution prevention, address space layout randomization, and bounds checking.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate supply chain risk management policy and procedures.
controls.full_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a supply chain risk management policy and procedures that address supply chain risks throughout the SDLC.

controls.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
controls.description: Develop a supply chain risk management plan for the system.
controls.full_text: The organization develops a plan for managing supply chain risks associated with the research and development, design, manufacturing, acquisition, delivery, integration, operations and maintenance, and disposal of the information system, system component, or information system service.

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
controls.description: Establish processes to identify and address weaknesses or deficiencies in the supply chain.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes a process or processes to identify and address weaknesses or deficiencies in the supply chain elements and processes in a timely manner.

controls.title: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
controls.description: Employ acquisition strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods to protect against supply chain risks.
controls.full_text: The organization employs organization-defined acquisition strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods to protect against, identify, and mitigate supply chain risks.

controls.title: Supplier Assessments and Reviews
controls.description: Assess and review the supply chain-related risks associated with suppliers or contractors.
controls.full_text: The organization assesses and reviews the supply chain-related risks associated with suppliers or contractors and the information system, system component, or information system service they provide at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Notification Agreements
controls.description: Establish agreements with suppliers for notification of supply chain compromises.
controls.full_text: The organization establishes agreements and procedures with entities involved in the supply chain for the information system, system component, or information system service for the notification of supply chain compromises and results of assessments or audits.
